name: Axios
what_it_does: Promise-based HTTP client for browser and Node.js. Provides interceptors for requests/responses, automatic JSON transformation, request cancellation, upload/download progress tracking, and a consistent API across browser (XMLHttpRequest) and Node.js (http module). The most popular HTTP client in the JavaScript ecosystem. Used when native fetch lacks features like interceptors, timeout handling, or progress events.
use_cases: Make HTTP requests with automatic JSON serialization/deserialization and consistent error handling across browser and Node.js
Implement request/response interceptors for authentication headers, logging, and error transformation
Handle upload/download progress events for file upload UIs that need progress bars
Cancel in-flight requests when components unmount or users navigate away using AbortController
Create reusable HTTP client instances with baseURL, timeout, and default headers configured once
not_for: Modern Node.js projects — native fetch is now available in Node.js 18+; axios overhead may not be worth it
Minimal bundle size requirements — axios adds ~15KB vs native fetch's 0KB overhead
Edge Runtime (Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge) — axios doesn't work in all edge environments; use native fetch
best_when: You need request/response interceptors, automatic JSON handling, and consistent cross-platform HTTP with a mature, well-documented API.
avoid_when: You're in a modern environment with native fetch and don't need interceptors — use fetch directly or a lightweight wrapper like ky.

auth.notes: HTTP client library — auth is implemented by application via request interceptors or manual header setting.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. Completely free. Community maintained. ~50M weekly npm downloads.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — HTTP client library.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Axios error objects include response, request, and config properties for debugging. HTTP errors throw AxiosError with status code. TypeScript types are comprehensive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests are idempotent. POST/PUT idempotency is application-level. Request deduplication not built-in — use TanStack Query for caching.
known_agent_gotchas: Axios throws on any non-2xx status code by default — use validateStatus option to customize what counts as an error
Axios does not deduplicate concurrent identical requests — use TanStack Query or SWR for request deduplication and caching
CancelToken is deprecated in favor of AbortController — use signal: controller.signal for request cancellation in new code
Content-Type is automatically set for objects — verify it's not overriding custom Content-Type headers for form submissions
CORS errors surface as 'Network Error' in browser — actual CORS configuration must be fixed on the server, not in Axios
Node.js adapter and browser adapter have different behaviors for streaming responses — verify streaming behavior per environment

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforcement is server responsibility. No CSRF protection built-in. Interceptors can add auth headers securely. MIT licensed. Widely audited.
